---
title: "1_Data"
author: "Arturo Bertero"
date: "2026-02-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#clean
rm(list = ls())

library("pacman")
p_load(tidyverse, here, haven, countrycode, readxl) 

```

# Input

```{r}
#load ess 11
raw_data <- read_dta(here("Input", "ESS11e04_1.dta"))
raw_data$clsprty
```

# Processing

## Select and rename

```{r}
# select and rename
lon_turn_df <- raw_data %>%
  select(
    # Design & weighting 
    cntry, 
    psu,      # primary sampling unit
    stratum,  # sampling stratum
    anweight, # weights
    
    # Dependent Variables
    vote = vote, # did u vote?
    starts_with("prtvt"), # Capture all country-specific party variables 
    # Plus the specific ones for de and lt
    any_of(c("prtvgde1", "prtvgde2", "prtvclt1", "prtvclt2", "prtvclt3")),
    
    # Loneliness & SI 
    lon = fltlnl,   # loneliness
    hhsize = hhmmb, # household size
    meet = sclmeet, # social meeting
    relig = rlgatnd, # attending religious services
    volunt = volunfp, # volunteering
    discuss = inprdsc, # how many ppl discuss with 
    unemployed = uempla, # unemployed
    
    # Controls & Interactions (Updated)
    gender = gndr,
    age = agea,
    educ = edulvlb,
    hhincome = hincfel,
    health = health,
    polint = polintr,   # Political interest (Interests in EU/Politics)
    partisanship = clsprty   # Particular party closeness (Partisanship proxy)
  )



```

## Var cleaning

```{r}
# 1. Master Processing and Recoding Chunk
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    # --- A: DVs & Party Recall ---
    reported_party_num = coalesce(
      zap_labels(prtvgde1), 
      zap_labels(prtvclt1),
      !!!lapply(select(., starts_with("prtv")), zap_labels)
    ),
    vote = case_when(
      vote == 1 ~ 1,
      vote == 2 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_ # Recodes 3 (Not eligible) and NAs to NA
    ),
    vote_strict = case_when(
      is.na(vote) ~ NA_real_,
      vote == 1 & !is.na(reported_party_num) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # --- B: Loneliness (Clean, Collapse, Numeric) ---
    lon_orig = as_factor(lon), 
    lon = fct_recode(lon_orig, NULL = "Refusal", NULL = "Don't know", NULL = "No answer") %>% fct_drop(),
    lon = fct_collapse(lon,
      "None/Almost none" = "None or almost none of the time",
      "Some of the time"  = "Some of the time",
      "Most/All the time" = c("Most of the time", "All or almost all of the time")
    ),
    lon = fct_relevel(lon, "None/Almost none", "Some of the time", "Most/All the time"),
    lon_n = as.numeric(lon),

    # --- C: Social Isolation (SI) Dummies (1 = Isolated) ---
    hhsize_iso = case_when(hhsize == 1 ~ 1, hhsize > 1 ~ 0, TRUE ~ NA_real_),
    meet_iso    = if_else(meet <= 2, 1, 0),
    relig_iso   = if_else(relig >= 5, 1, 0),
    volunt_iso  = if_else(volunt == 2, 1, 0),
    discuss_iso = if_else(discuss <= 1, 1, 0),
    unemp_iso   = if_else(unemployed == 1, 1, 0),

    # --- D: Revision Variables (Partisanship & Pol Int) ---
    partisanship = case_when(
      partisanship == 1 ~ 1,
      partisanship == 2 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_ # Handles Refusals and Don't knows
    ),
    
    # Reverse ESS scale once: 1=Low, 4=High [cite: 2026-02-05]
    polint_n = 5 - as.numeric(polint),
    
    # Restore the descriptive factor with the new logical order
    polint = factor(polint_n, 
                    levels = 1:4, 
                    labels = c("Not at all", "Hardly", "Quite", "Very")),
    
    # Center the correctly flipped numeric variable
    polint_centered = polint_n - mean(polint_n, na.rm = TRUE),

    # --- E: Controls ---
    gender = as_factor(gender) %>% fct_recode(NULL = "No answer") %>% fct_drop(),
    health = as_factor(health),
    hhincome = as_factor(hhincome) %>% fct_recode(NULL = "Don't know", NULL = "Refusal", NULL = "No answer") %>% fct_drop(),
    
    # Education 3-level grouping
    educ_raw = as_factor(educ),
    educ = case_when(
      educ_raw %in% c("Not completed ISCED level 1", 
                  "ISCED 1, completed primary education",
                  "General ISCED 2A, access ISCED 3A general/all 3",
                  "Vocational ISCED 2", "Vocational ISCED 2A/2B", "Vocational ISCED 2C") ~ "Low",
      str_detect(as.character(educ_raw), "ISCED 3|ISCED 4") ~ "Medium",
      str_detect(as.character(educ_raw), "ISCED 5|ISCED 6") ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"))
  ) %>%
  
  # 2. Final Variable Selection
  select(
    cntry:anweight, # ESS design vars
    vote, vote_strict, # DVs
    lon_orig, lon, lon_n, # Loneliness
    hhsize_iso, meet_iso, relig_iso, volunt_iso, discuss_iso, unemp_iso, # SI markers
    partisanship, polint, polint_n, polint_centered, # Revision variables
    gender, age, educ, hhincome, health # Controls
  )

# 3. Quick Inspection
lon_turn_df %>%
  select(vote, lon, polint_n, educ) %>%
  map(~table(.x, useNA = "always"))
```



## SI index and SIL typology

```{r}
# 1. Build the additive index (Original 0-7 scale)
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    si_index_raw = rowSums(select(., ends_with("_iso")), na.rm = FALSE)
  )

# 2. Print the problematic raw table (to document why we are recoding)
# This serves as your "Evidence of Sparsity" for reviewers
print("Raw SI Index Distribution (Problematic):")
table(lon_turn_df$cntry, lon_turn_df$si_index_raw)

# 3. Recode into 3 Levels: 0-1 (Low), 2-3 (Mid), 4+ (High)
# 1. Recode SI into the 3 proposed levels
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    si_levels = case_when(
      si_index_raw <= 1 ~ "0-1: Low ISO",
      si_index_raw == 2 ~ "2: Mid ISO",
      si_index_raw >= 3 ~ "3+: High ISO",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("0-1: Low ISO", "2: Mid ISO", "3+: High ISO"))
  )

# 2. Check the new distribution by country
# This should show much healthier 'High' cells for CH, IS, and SE
print("Revised 3-Level SI Distribution (0-1, 2, 3+):")
table(lon_turn_df$cntry, lon_turn_df$si_levels)
```

```{r}
# SI-L
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  mutate(
    lon_n = as.numeric(lon),
    si_n  = as.numeric(si_index_raw),
    
    # Calculate thresholds
    lon_threshold = median(lon_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    si_threshold  = median(si_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # BALANCED LOGIC:
    # We use '<=' for Low and '>' for High.
    # This ensures that even if the median is the lowest value (1), 
    # the 'Low' group gets populated.
    sil_type = case_when(
      lon_n <= lon_threshold & si_n <= si_threshold ~ "Low L, Low SI",
      lon_n <= lon_threshold & si_n >  si_threshold ~ "Low L, High SI",
      lon_n >  lon_threshold & si_n <= si_threshold ~ "High L, Low SI",
      lon_n >  lon_threshold & si_n >  si_threshold ~ "High L, High SI",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Low L, Low SI", "Low L, High SI", 
                            "High L, Low SI", "High L, High SI"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Audit the new distribution
table(lon_turn_df$sil_type)
table(lon_turn_df$cntry, lon_turn_df$sil_type)
```

## Need for imputation?

```{r}
# 1. Variable-level Missingness (Percentages)
miss_summary <- lon_turn_df %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.x)) / n() * 100)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Percent_Missing") %>%
  arrange(desc(Percent_Missing))

print(miss_summary)
```


```{r}
# 2. Case-level Missingness (How many variables is each person missing?)
# This helps us identify "bad" respondents who skipped half the survey
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(miss_count = rowSums(is.na(select(., -starts_with("vote")))))

table(lon_turn_df$miss_count)
```


```{r}
# 3. Country-level Audit
# This is crucial because ESS data quality varies by national context 
country_miss <- lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_miss = mean(is.na(vote_strict) | is.na(lon) | is.na(hhsize_iso)),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_miss))

print(country_miss)
```

## Center vars

```{r}
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Age: Already centered relative to country mean
    age_centered = age - mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 2. Political Interest: Center for interaction terms (Revision requirement)
    # Higher value = More interest (1-4 scale) [cite: 2026-02-05]
    polint_centered = polint_n - mean(polint_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 3. Loneliness: Center the 3-level numeric scale
    lon_centered = lon_n - mean(lon_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 4. Social Isolation: Center the raw 0-6 index
    si_centered = si_n - mean(si_n, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Quick check: All means should be approx 0
lon_turn_df %>%
  select(ends_with("_centered")) %>%
  summary()
```
## Merging with turnout

```{r}
# --- 1. Import and Process IDEA Official Turnout Data ---
# We want the second-to-last election to act as a lagged control
idea_lagged <- read_excel(here("Input", "idea.xlsx")) %>%
  select(ISO2, year, Voter_Turnout = `Voter Turnout`) %>%
  mutate(
    official_turnout_lag = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", Voter_Turnout)) / 100,
    election_year = as.numeric(substr(year, 1, 4))
  ) %>%
  group_by(ISO2) %>%
  # Logic: Rank by year and pick the 2nd most recent
  arrange(desc(election_year)) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number()) %>%
  filter(rank == 2) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  select(cntry = ISO2, official_turnout_lag)

# --- 2. Merge with main dataset ---
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  left_join(idea_lagged, by = "cntry")

# --- 3. Final Check before Saving ---
# Ensure countries like BE (Belgium) have their high turnout lag recorded
lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(lag = mean(official_turnout_lag)) %>%
  print(n = Inf)
```


# Output

```{r}
# Export to RDS
saveRDS(lon_turn_df, here("Input", "data_clean.rds"))
```
